title: Alpha Lipoic Acid IV & Injections: Benefits, Uses & What to Expect
excerpt: How ALA IV therapy and IM injections support nerve health, blood sugar, liver detox, mitochondrial energy, and antioxidant defense.
content:
Alpha lipoic acid (ALA) is one of the most versatile antioxidants in integrative medicine. Unlike most antioxidants that work only in water or only in fat, ALA is both water- and fat-soluble — meaning it can neutralize free radicals throughout the body, cross the blood-brain barrier, and recycle other antioxidants like vitamin C, vitamin E, glutathione, and CoQ10.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine in Newport Beach and Rancho Cucamonga, we offer alpha lipoic acid as both an IV infusion and an intramuscular (IM) injection to give patients flexible, high-bioavailability options for nerve support, blood sugar regulation, liver detox, and cellular energy.

Explore our full IV therapy menu or learn about IV glutathione — a natural pairing with ALA for advanced antioxidant support.

What Is Alpha Lipoic Acid?

Alpha lipoic acid is a naturally occurring compound made in small amounts by your mitochondria. It plays a critical role in converting glucose into energy (ATP) and protecting cells from oxidative damage. While ALA is found in trace amounts in foods like spinach, broccoli, and organ meats, dietary intake is not enough to produce therapeutic effects — which is why supplementation, IV therapy, and injections are used clinically.

Alpha Lipoic Acid Benefits

1. Nerve Health & Neuropathy Support

ALA is one of the most well-researched therapies for peripheral and diabetic neuropathy. Studies show it can reduce burning, tingling, numbness, and pain by decreasing oxidative stress in nerve tissue and improving nerve conduction. IV ALA has been used in European clinics for decades as a first-line neuropathy therapy.

2. Blood Sugar & Insulin Sensitivity

Alpha lipoic acid helps improve insulin sensitivity and supports healthier post-meal blood sugar levels. It is often used as part of an integrative metabolic protocol for patients with insulin resistance, prediabetes, PCOS, or metabolic syndrome. Patients on diabetes medications should be monitored closely, as ALA can enhance the effect of these drugs.

3. Liver Detoxification & Heavy Metal Support

ALA supports phase II liver detoxification and binds to certain heavy metals, helping the body eliminate mercury, lead, and other toxins more safely. It is a common adjunct in mold exposure and toxin-burden protocols.

4. Mitochondrial Energy Production

Because ALA is a cofactor for key mitochondrial enzymes, it directly supports ATP production — your cellular energy currency. Patients with chronic fatigue, brain fog, or post-viral depletion often report improved energy and mental clarity with regular ALA therapy.

5. Cardiovascular & Vascular Health

ALA improves endothelial function (the health of your blood vessel lining), reduces oxidative stress on LDL cholesterol, and supports healthy blood pressure — making it a valuable therapy in cardiovascular and longevity protocols.

6. Skin & Anti-Aging

As a potent antioxidant that recycles vitamin C, vitamin E, and glutathione, ALA helps protect skin cells from free radical damage and supports collagen integrity. Many patients notice improved skin tone and clarity after several weeks of IV ALA combined with glutathione.

IV vs IM Injection: Which Is Right for You?

Both delivery methods bypass the digestive tract for far greater bioavailability than oral ALA supplements. The right choice depends on your goals, condition, and schedule.

Alpha Lipoic Acid IV

  • Delivers a higher therapeutic dose (typically 300–600 mg) over 30 to 60 minutes
  • Preferred for neuropathy, detox protocols, and metabolic support
  • Can be combined with glutathione, vitamin C, or a full nutrient IV
  • Ideal for loading-phase protocols (1–2x/week for several weeks)

Alpha Lipoic Acid IM Injection

  • Quick in-and-out — takes just a few minutes
  • Great for ongoing maintenance and energy or antioxidant support
  • Convenient option between IV sessions or for patients who prefer injections
  • Can be combined with other vitamin and nutrient injections like B12, glutathione, or NAD+

What to Expect During an ALA Session

For an IV session, you'll relax in a comfortable treatment chair while the infusion runs over 30 to 60 minutes. Most patients read, work, or rest during their appointment. There is no downtime afterward.

For an IM injection, the visit is quick — usually less than 10 minutes total. The injection is given in the deltoid or gluteal muscle with minimal discomfort.

Who Is Alpha Lipoic Acid Best For?

  • Patients with peripheral or diabetic neuropathy
  • Insulin resistance, prediabetes, PCOS, or metabolic syndrome
  • Chronic fatigue, brain fog, or mitochondrial dysfunction
  • Heavy metal or environmental toxin burden
  • Post-illness recovery and long COVID
  • Longevity, cardiovascular, and anti-aging optimization

Is Alpha Lipoic Acid Safe?

Alpha lipoic acid is well tolerated by most patients. Because it can enhance the blood sugar-lowering effect of diabetes medications, patients on insulin or oral hypoglycemics should be monitored closely. Rare side effects can include mild nausea or a temporary drop in blood sugar. Our providers screen every patient before starting therapy.

Combining ALA with Other Integrative Therapies

ALA works synergistically with several therapies we offer:

  • IV glutathione — for advanced antioxidant and detox support
  • NAD+ IV therapy — for mitochondrial and cognitive support
  • Comprehensive lab testing — to assess metabolic and oxidative stress markers
  • Mitochondrial peptides like MOTS-c and SS-31

Every ALA protocol at Reclaim Integrative Medicine is customized to your labs, symptoms, and goals — because antioxidant therapy works best when it's personalized.

title: B12 Injections: Benefits, Forms, and What to Expect
excerpt: A naturopathic guide to vitamin B12 shots — energy, brain fog, mood, methylcobalamin vs hydroxocobalamin, dosing, and who benefits most.
content:
If you are dragging through your afternoons, battling brain fog, or feeling like your energy never quite catches up to your life — your vitamin B12 status deserves a closer look. B12 is one of the most common nutrient deficiencies we see in clinic, and one of the easiest to correct quickly with targeted B12 injections.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine in Newport Beach and Rancho Cucamonga, we use physician-prescribed methylcobalamin and hydroxocobalamin injections as part of personalized naturopathic protocols. This guide breaks down what B12 actually does, who benefits most, the differences between forms, and what to expect from a B12 shot program.

Want to see all of our injectable options? Visit our vitamin & nutrient injections page or explore IV therapy for full-spectrum nutrient repletion.

What Is Vitamin B12?

Vitamin B12 (cobalamin) is a water-soluble vitamin essential for almost every system in the body. It is required for:

  • Red blood cell formation — without B12, cells become enlarged and inefficient (megaloblastic anemia)
  • Nervous system function — B12 maintains the myelin sheath that protects nerves
  • DNA synthesis — needed for cellular replication and repair
  • Methylation — a core biochemical process that regulates detoxification, hormone balance, and gene expression
  • Energy production — B12 helps convert food into ATP, the body's cellular fuel
  • Mood and cognition — required for neurotransmitter synthesis including serotonin and dopamine

Your body cannot make B12 on its own. It comes from animal foods (meat, fish, eggs, dairy) and from supplementation. Absorption depends on a healthy stomach, adequate intrinsic factor, and a functioning small intestine — which is exactly why deficiency is so common.

Why B12 Deficiency Is So Common

Even people who eat plenty of B12-rich foods can be deficient. Common culprits include:

  • Low stomach acid from aging, stress, or long-term acid blocker use (PPIs, H2 blockers)
  • Metformin and other medications that interfere with absorption
  • Vegetarian or vegan diets with no reliable B12 source
  • Digestive conditions like celiac, Crohn's, SIBO, or post-gastric bypass
  • Thyroid and autoimmune conditions (pernicious anemia, Hashimoto's)
  • MTHFR and other methylation gene variants that impair B12 utilization
  • Chronic stress and high alcohol intake

Standard lab reference ranges often miss functional B12 deficiency. Patients with "normal" serum B12 can still have low cellular B12 — which is why we look at methylmalonic acid, homocysteine, and clinical symptoms as part of our comprehensive lab workup.

Signs You May Benefit from B12 Injections

  • Persistent fatigue or low stamina, even with adequate sleep
  • Brain fog, poor concentration, or memory lapses
  • Numbness, tingling, or "pins and needles" in hands or feet
  • Low mood, anxiety, or irritability
  • Pale skin or shortness of breath with mild activity
  • Glossitis (smooth, sore tongue) or mouth ulcers
  • Hair thinning or weakened nails
  • Difficulty losing weight despite diet and exercise
  • Recovery from illness, surgery, or post-viral fatigue

These overlap with many other conditions — thyroid issues, hormonal imbalances, and chronic fatigue — which is why we evaluate the full picture before recommending a protocol.

Why Injections Outperform Oral B12

Oral B12 has to survive stomach acid, bind to intrinsic factor, and be absorbed in the terminal ileum. For patients with gut issues, low intrinsic factor, or medication interference, oral absorption can drop to under 1%. B12 injections bypass the gut entirely, delivering the vitamin directly into muscle tissue for rapid, near-complete absorption.

For patients who are highly symptomatic, deficient on labs, or recovering from illness, injections produce noticeably faster results than capsules or sublinguals.

Methylcobalamin vs. Hydroxocobalamin vs. Cyanocobalamin

Not all B12 is created equal. The form matters.

Methylcobalamin

The active, methylated form of B12. It is immediately usable by the body — no conversion required. Methylcobalamin is our preferred form for patients with:

  • MTHFR gene variants
  • Neurological symptoms (tingling, brain fog, neuropathy)
  • Mood and methylation concerns
  • Chronic fatigue

Hydroxocobalamin

A longer-acting form that the body slowly converts into the active forms. It stays in circulation longer, making it an excellent choice for sustained repletion and for patients with toxin exposure (it binds to cyanide and supports detox pathways).

Cyanocobalamin

A synthetic form that contains a cyanide molecule (in trace, non-toxic amounts) and must be converted by the body. It is the cheapest and most common form found in mass-market supplements — but it is not our preference in clinic.

What to Expect at Your Appointment

  • Brief intake: New patients complete a short health history. Existing patients can typically come in for an injection-only visit.
  • Form selection: Our provider recommends methylcobalamin or hydroxocobalamin based on your symptoms, labs, and methylation status.
  • The injection: A small intramuscular shot in the deltoid (upper arm) or gluteal muscle. The entire visit usually takes under 15 minutes.
  • Aftercare: No downtime. You can return to work, the gym, or your day immediately.

How Often Should You Get B12 Injections?

Loading Phase

For patients who are deficient or highly symptomatic, we typically start with weekly injections for 4 to 8 weeks. This restores tissue stores and produces the most dramatic symptom improvement.

Maintenance Phase

Once levels and symptoms stabilize, most patients transition to bi-weekly or monthly injections to maintain optimal status — especially vegans, older adults, and patients on long-term medications that block absorption.

As-Needed

Some patients use B12 injections situationally — before travel, during high-stress weeks, after illness, or when stamina demands spike. Even a single well-timed injection can deliver meaningful benefit.

B12 and Weight Loss

B12 alone is not a weight loss drug — but adequate B12 is foundational for the metabolism, energy, and motivation that sustain a successful program. B12 is also a common addition to MIC (lipotropic) injections used to support fat metabolism. For patients on a comprehensive plan, see our weight management services.

Is B12 Safe?

Yes. B12 has one of the strongest safety profiles of any nutrient. It is water-soluble, so excess is excreted in urine. Side effects are rare and typically limited to:

  • Mild soreness or bruising at the injection site
  • Temporary warmth or flushing
  • A brief metallic taste

Patients with rare cobalt allergies or specific kidney conditions should be evaluated before treatment. Our providers screen for contraindications at your first visit.

Who Should Consider B12 Injections?

  • Vegans and vegetarians
  • Adults 50+ with declining stomach acid production
  • Patients on PPIs, H2 blockers, or metformin
  • Patients with thyroid, autoimmune, or gut conditions
  • Anyone struggling with low energy, brain fog, or mood
  • Athletes and high performers seeking faster recovery
  • Post-viral, post-surgical, or post-illness recovery
  • Patients with MTHFR or methylation variants

The Reclaim Approach to B12

We do not hand out one-size-fits-all B12 shots. Every patient is evaluated with appropriate labs, a thorough symptom review, and a personalized plan that may include nutritional support, methylation testing, and complementary therapies such as IV glutathione, NAD+ IV therapy, or mitochondrial support protocols.

Our goal is not just to refill a tank — it is to address the why behind your deficiency so you stay well long-term.

title: IV Glutathione: Benefits, Skin Brightening & What to Expect
excerpt: Learn what IV glutathione therapy is, how it works for skin brightening, detox, energy, and immune support, and how often to get it.
content:
Glutathione is often called the "master antioxidant" — and for good reason. Found in every cell of your body, this tripeptide molecule plays a central role in neutralizing free radicals, detoxifying harmful compounds, supporting immune function, and maintaining cellular health. While your body produces glutathione naturally, levels decline with age, stress, poor diet, environmental toxins, and chronic illness.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine in Newport Beach and Rancho Cucamonga, we offer physician-supervised IV glutathione therapy to restore optimal levels quickly and effectively. Whether you are seeking brighter skin, deeper detoxification, or enhanced energy and immunity, this guide covers everything you need to know about glutathione IV therapy.

Looking for our full range of IV options? Explore IV therapy services or learn about advanced IV protocols including NAD+ and Plaquex.

What Is Glutathione?

Glutathione is a small protein made from three amino acids: cysteine, glycine, and glutamate. It exists in two forms inside your cells — reduced (active) and oxidized (inactive). The reduced form does the heavy lifting: scavenging free radicals, binding to toxins for safe elimination, and regenerating other antioxidants like vitamin C and vitamin E.

Glutathione works best when paired with cofactors and synergistic antioxidants — one of the most powerful is alpha lipoic acid (ALA) IV and IM therapy, which regenerates glutathione inside the cell and amplifies its detox effects.

Your liver produces the majority of your body's glutathione, using it to process everything from alcohol and medications to environmental pollutants and metabolic waste. When glutathione levels drop, detoxification slows, oxidative stress rises, and cellular damage accelerates.

Why IV Glutathione Instead of Oral Supplements?

Oral glutathione supplements face a major obstacle: digestive breakdown. Stomach acid and intestinal enzymes rapidly degrade glutathione, and what little survives must then pass through the intestinal wall and survive first-pass liver metabolism. Clinical studies show that oral glutathione raises blood levels only modestly, if at all.

IV glutathione bypasses the digestive system entirely, delivering the molecule directly into your bloodstream at 100% bioavailability. From there, it circulates to every tissue — skin, brain, liver, lungs, and immune cells — where it can go to work immediately.

For patients with:

  • Digestive conditions that impair nutrient absorption
  • High toxic burden from mold, metals, or environmental exposure
  • Chronic fatigue or mitochondrial dysfunction
  • Skin concerns seeking brightening and antioxidant support
  • Immune challenges or autoimmune conditions

IV delivery is the most reliable and effective option.

IV Glutathione Benefits

Patients seek glutathione IV therapy for a wide range of health and aesthetic goals. Here are the most well-documented benefits:

1. Skin Brightening & Radiance

One of the most popular reasons patients choose IV glutathione is for skin brightening. Glutathione inhibits tyrosinase, the enzyme responsible for melanin production. Over a series of treatments, many patients notice a more even skin tone, reduced hyperpigmentation, and a natural glow. It also protects skin cells from UV-induced oxidative damage, supporting long-term skin health.

2. Powerful Cellular Detoxification

Your liver relies on glutathione to neutralize and eliminate toxins. IV glutathione supports phase II detoxification — the process that makes fat-soluble toxins water-soluble so they can be excreted. This is especially valuable for patients dealing with:

  • Mold or mycotoxin exposure
  • Heavy metal burden
  • Chronic medication use
  • Environmental chemical exposure
  • Post-illness recovery

3. Antioxidant Defense & Anti-Aging

Oxidative stress — an imbalance between free radicals and antioxidants — is a root cause of cellular aging, inflammation, and chronic disease. Glutathione is your body's first line of defense. By neutralizing free radicals and recycling other antioxidants, it protects DNA, proteins, and cell membranes from damage. For patients focused on longevity medicine, glutathione is a foundational therapy.

4. Immune System Support

Glutathione is essential for proper immune cell function. It helps T-cells proliferate, supports natural killer (NK) cell activity, and modulates inflammatory cytokine production. Patients with autoimmune conditions, chronic infections, or weakened immunity often benefit from regular glutathione IV sessions as part of a broader immune-support protocol.

5. Energy & Mitochondrial Health

Mitochondria — your cellular power plants — are highly vulnerable to oxidative damage. Glutathione protects mitochondrial membranes and supports ATP production. Patients with chronic fatigue, fibromyalgia, or post-viral depletion often report improved energy and reduced brain fog after a series of glutathione infusions.

6. Brain Health & Neuroprotection

The brain is particularly susceptible to oxidative stress due to its high metabolic demand and lipid-rich tissue. Glutathione crosses the blood-brain barrier and helps protect neurons from damage. It has been studied for its potential role in supporting cognitive health, reducing brain fog, and protecting against neurodegenerative processes.

What to Expect During Your IV Glutathione Session

A typical glutathione IV session at Reclaim Integrative Medicine is straightforward and comfortable:

  • Check-in: You'll arrive at our Newport Beach or Rancho Cucamonga clinic, where our team reviews your health history and confirms your protocol.
  • IV placement: A small IV catheter is inserted into a vein in your arm — similar to a blood draw. The sensation is a brief pinch.
  • The infusion: Glutathione is administered slowly over 15 to 30 minutes. You'll relax in a comfortable treatment chair. Many patients read, scroll on their phone, or simply rest.
  • Post-session: There is no downtime. Most patients feel refreshed and energized immediately. You can resume normal activities right away.

When glutathione is combined with a full nutrient IV drip, the total session lasts 45 to 90 minutes depending on the formula.

IV Glutathione Protocol: How Often Should You Get It?

The ideal frequency depends on your goals, current health status, and budget.

Loading Phase

For patients seeking noticeable results — whether for skin brightening, detox support, or energy recovery — we typically recommend a loading phase of weekly sessions for 4 to 6 weeks. This builds up cellular glutathione stores and delivers the most dramatic initial benefits.

Maintenance Phase

After the loading phase, most patients transition to bi-weekly or monthly maintenance infusions to sustain optimal levels. Skin-brightening patients often prefer monthly sessions to maintain their results.

Event-Based or As-Needed

Some patients use glutathione IV on an as-needed basis — before travel, after illness, during high-stress periods, or before a special event where they want their skin to glow. While less effective than a structured protocol, a single infusion still delivers meaningful antioxidant and detox support.

Is IV Glutathione Safe?

Yes — IV glutathione is generally very safe when administered by trained medical professionals in a clinical setting. Glutathione is a molecule your body already produces naturally; IV therapy simply replenishes levels that have declined.

Side effects are rare and typically mild:

  • Light flushing or warmth
  • Mild headache
  • Metallic taste during infusion
  • Lightheadedness (usually from dehydration)

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, our providers monitor every session and adjust the protocol in real time for your comfort and safety. IV glutathione may not be appropriate for patients with certain conditions; we conduct a brief health screening before your first session.

Who Is IV Glutathione Best For?

IV glutathione therapy is ideal for patients who:

  • Want brighter, more radiant skin and even tone
  • Are dealing with high toxic burden or mold exposure
  • Experience persistent fatigue or brain fog
  • Have chronic conditions involving oxidative stress
  • Want immune support during cold/flu season or travel
  • Are focused on longevity and anti-aging optimization
  • Are athletes or active individuals seeking faster recovery

IV Glutathione vs. Other Delivery Methods

While IV is the gold standard for glutathione delivery, other forms exist:

  • Oral liposomal glutathione: Better absorbed than standard capsules, but still less reliable than IV. Useful for daily maintenance between IV sessions.
  • Glutathione nebulizer: Inhaled directly into the lungs. Useful for respiratory conditions but limited systemic absorption.
  • Topical glutathione: Minimal absorption through the skin. Cosmetic benefits are limited compared to IV.

For patients seeking maximum therapeutic benefit — whether for detox, skin brightening, energy, or immune support — IV administration remains the most effective and well-studied delivery method.

Glutathione as Part of a Comprehensive Wellness Plan

IV glutathione works best as part of a broader integrative protocol. During your consultation, we may recommend combining glutathione with:

  • Vitamin IV therapy for comprehensive nutrient repletion
  • NAD+ IV therapy for mitochondrial and cognitive support
  • Comprehensive lab testing to assess oxidative stress and detox pathways
  • Targeted supplementation and dietary modifications to support endogenous glutathione production

Our naturopathic approach ensures that every treatment is tailored to your unique biochemistry, health history, and goals.

title: Mitochondrial Health: How Peptides & IV Therapy Support Cellular Energy
excerpt: Mitochondrial dysfunction drives fatigue, brain fog, and aging. Learn how MOTS-c, SS-31, NAD+ IV, and glutathione protocols restore cellular energy from the inside out.
content:
Your mitochondria are the tiny power plants inside nearly every cell of your body. They generate the ATP that fuels your brain, muscles, heart, and immune system. When mitochondria falter, you feel it everywhere — persistent fatigue, brain fog, slow recovery, stalled metabolism, and accelerated aging.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, mitochondrial support is a cornerstone of our longevity medicine and chronic fatigue programs. Two of the most effective tools we use are targeted peptides and IV therapy.

What Are Mitochondria and Why Do They Matter?

Mitochondria convert the food you eat and the oxygen you breathe into ATP — the energy currency of every cell. Beyond energy production, mitochondria also regulate:

  • Cellular signaling — they decide whether a damaged cell repairs or self-destructs
  • Calcium balance — critical for nerve and muscle function
  • Hormone synthesis — especially steroid hormones like cortisol, testosterone, and estrogen
  • Detoxification — clearing reactive oxygen species (ROS) and metabolic waste
  • Inflammation control — dysfunctional mitochondria drive chronic inflammation

Signs of Mitochondrial Dysfunction

  • Persistent fatigue that doesn't improve with rest
  • Brain fog, poor focus, and memory lapses
  • Exercise intolerance and slow recovery from workouts
  • Muscle weakness or aches
  • Unexplained weight gain or metabolic resistance
  • Mood changes, anxiety, or depression
  • Premature aging, low libido, and hormonal decline

Mitochondrial dysfunction is often the hidden driver behind chronic fatigue, fibromyalgia, long COVID, autoimmune flares, and neurodegenerative conditions.

Peptides That Support Mitochondrial Function

Peptide therapy has emerged as one of the most precise ways to repair and protect mitochondria. A few of our most-used peptides for mitochondrial support:

MOTS-c

MOTS-c is a mitochondrial-derived peptide — meaning your mitochondria actually make it themselves. Levels decline with age. Supplementing MOTS-c has been shown to improve insulin sensitivity, enhance exercise capacity, support fat metabolism, and protect against age-related metabolic decline.

SS-31 (Elamipretide)

SS-31 targets the inner mitochondrial membrane, where ATP is produced. It stabilizes cardiolipin (a key membrane lipid), reduces oxidative stress, and restores efficient energy production. It's especially valuable for patients with chronic fatigue, heart-related symptoms, or neurodegenerative concerns.

Humanin

Another mitochondrial-derived peptide, Humanin protects cells from stress-induced death and is being studied for its role in cognitive aging, metabolic health, and cardiovascular protection.

BPC-157 and the Wolverine Stack

While best known for tissue healing, BPC-157 also supports mitochondrial biogenesis (creating new mitochondria) and reduces oxidative damage — making it useful for recovery, gut healing, and overall cellular resilience.

IV Therapy for Mitochondrial Repair

IV therapy delivers nutrients directly into the bloodstream at concentrations impossible to reach with oral supplements. For mitochondrial support, several IV protocols stand out:

NAD+ IV Therapy

NAD+ is essential for the electron transport chain — the final step in ATP production. NAD+ levels fall sharply with age, and IV NAD+ is the fastest way to restore them. Patients commonly report better energy, sharper focus, and improved recovery within days of a loading protocol.

Glutathione

Glutathione is your body's master antioxidant. It neutralizes the reactive oxygen species mitochondria generate as a byproduct of energy production. IV glutathione protects mitochondrial membranes and supports detoxification pathways that often become overwhelmed in chronic illness.

Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA)

A mitochondrial cofactor and one of the few antioxidants that works in both water- and fat-soluble compartments. Alpha lipoic acid IV and IM injection therapy supports ATP production, recycles glutathione and CoQ10, and is especially helpful for patients with neuropathy, insulin resistance, or heavy metal burden.

B Vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B5, B6, B12)

Every B vitamin plays a direct role in mitochondrial metabolism. Many patients with fatigue are functionally depleted even when serum levels look "normal." IV B-complex bypasses absorption issues and restores cofactors needed for ATP production.

CoQ10, Magnesium, and Alpha-Lipoic Acid

Added to custom IV protocols, these nutrients directly support the electron transport chain, calm oxidative stress, and improve insulin signaling.

A Combined Protocol for Cellular Energy

The most powerful mitochondrial programs layer peptides and IV therapy together. A typical approach we use at Reclaim:

  • Phase 1 — Repletion: NAD+ IV loading (2–4 sessions) plus glutathione and B-complex
  • Phase 2 — Repair: SS-31 or MOTS-c peptide therapy alongside maintenance IV drips
  • Phase 3 — Maintenance: Monthly IVs, ongoing peptide cycles, and nutrient and hormone optimization based on comprehensive labs

Lifestyle Foundations Still Matter

Peptides and IVs accelerate results, but they work best when paired with the basics: strength training, zone-2 cardio, quality sleep, time-restricted eating, sunlight exposure, and minimizing exposure to mold, heavy metals, and ultra-processed foods.

Frequently Asked Questions About Mitochondrial Health

What causes mitochondrial dysfunction?

Common triggers include chronic infections, toxin exposure (mold, heavy metals), nutrient deficiencies, poor sleep, unmanaged stress, sedentary lifestyle, and the natural aging process. Autoimmune conditions and chronic inflammation also damage mitochondrial membranes over time. Many patients who come to us through our chronic fatigue program discover mitochondrial dysfunction is the root cause of their symptoms.

How do I know if I have mitochondrial dysfunction?

While standard bloodwork often looks "normal," clues include fatigue unrelieved by rest, exercise intolerance, brain fog, and slow recovery. At Reclaim, we use comprehensive functional testing to assess mitochondrial markers, organic acids, and nutrient cofactors.

Are peptides safe for supporting mitochondria?

When prescribed and monitored by a qualified naturopathic physician, peptide therapy options like MOTS-c, SS-31, and BPC-157 have strong safety profiles. They are administered in precise doses with individualized protocols based on your health history and lab results.

How quickly does IV NAD+ therapy work?

Many patients notice improved energy and mental clarity after their first 1–2 NAD+ IV therapy sessions. A typical loading protocol involves 4–6 sessions over 2–3 weeks, followed by monthly maintenance depending on response and goals.

Can lifestyle changes really improve mitochondrial function?

Absolutely. Zone-2 cardiovascular exercise, resistance training, time-restricted eating, quality sleep, and reducing environmental toxin exposure all stimulate mitochondrial biogenesis — the creation of new, healthy mitochondria. These foundations are a core part of our longevity medicine approach.

Is mitochondrial support only for older patients?

No. Mitochondrial dysfunction is increasingly seen in younger patients due to chronic stress, poor diet, infections, and environmental toxins. Supporting cellular energy benefits patients of all ages, from athletes seeking recovery to professionals battling burnout. Our longevity medicine and chronic fatigue programs serve patients across the age spectrum.

Restore Your Cellular Energy in Newport Beach

If you've been told your labs are "normal" but you still feel exhausted, foggy, or older than your years, mitochondrial dysfunction may be at the root. Our team in Newport Beach and Rancho Cucamonga builds personalized protocols using peptides, IV therapy, hormone optimization, and advanced testing to help you feel like yourself again.

title: Low Testosterone & TRT in Newport Beach: A Naturopathic Guide for Men
excerpt: Signs of low T, the labs that matter, TRT delivery options, and what naturopathic testosterone replacement looks like for men in Newport Beach and Orange County.
content:
If you're a man in Newport Beach, Orange County, or Rancho Cucamonga noticing fatigue, declining strength, low libido, brain fog, or stubborn belly fat that wasn't there a few years ago, low testosterone may be a driver. Testosterone replacement therapy (TRT) — done correctly, with comprehensive labs and naturopathic oversight — can restore energy, body composition, mood, and sexual function. Done poorly, it can leave you dependent on a hormone that was never properly diagnosed.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, our naturopathic doctors evaluate the full hormonal picture before prescribing — and we treat the root cause when one exists. Here's what every man considering TRT in Orange County should understand.

Signs of Low Testosterone in Men

  • Persistent fatigue and reduced workout recovery
  • Loss of muscle mass and grip strength
  • Increased abdominal/visceral fat
  • Low libido, weaker morning erections, or erectile difficulty
  • Mood changes — irritability, low motivation, mild depression
  • Brain fog, poor concentration, memory slips
  • Disrupted sleep or new-onset sleep apnea symptoms
  • Reduced beard/body hair growth or thinning

What Labs Are Needed Before Starting TRT?

A trustworthy TRT evaluation is more than a single total testosterone number. Our standard men's hormone panel includes:

  • Total and free testosterone (drawn before 10 AM, ideally fasted)
  • SHBG, albumin, and estradiol (sensitive assay)
  • LH and FSH — to distinguish primary vs. secondary hypogonadism
  • DHEA-S, prolactin, cortisol
  • Complete thyroid panel — low thyroid mimics low T
  • CBC, CMP, lipid panel, hs-CRP, HbA1c, fasting insulin
  • PSA and a digital exam for men 40+
  • Vitamin D, ferritin, B12, homocysteine

Comprehensive lab testing tells us whether your low T is a primary hormone problem, a downstream effect of poor sleep or metabolic disease, or something correctable without lifelong therapy.

TRT Delivery Methods: Injections, Pellets, Creams

  • Testosterone cypionate injections — most common, dosed weekly or twice-weekly for stable levels.
  • Bioidentical pellets (Biote) — inserted under the skin every 3–6 months for hands-off convenience.
  • Transdermal cream — daily application, gentler ramp-up, easier to titrate.
  • Enclomiphene — for younger men who want to preserve fertility and stimulate their own production.

We match the delivery method to your goals, fertility plans, and lifestyle. See our full hormone replacement overview or the dedicated TRT page.

What Naturopathic TRT Adds

Conventional TRT clinics often prescribe testosterone and stop there. Naturopathic TRT goes further:

  • Estradiol management without reflexively crushing estrogen (you need some)
  • Fertility-preserving protocols using HCG or enclomiphene when appropriate
  • Targeted support for sleep, insulin sensitivity, and visceral fat
  • Optional pairing with peptide therapy, recovery peptides, or IV therapy
  • Lifestyle, nutrition, and resistance-training guidance built into the plan

TRT Membership in Orange County

For ongoing care we offer a TRT membership that bundles medication, monitoring labs, and provider visits at a predictable monthly rate — a better fit for most men than fee-for-service.

title: The Best Peptides for Men: BPC-157, Tesamorelin, Ipamorelin & More

content:
Peptide therapy has become one of the most powerful tools in men's longevity medicine. Used correctly, peptides can accelerate injury recovery, reduce visceral fat, boost natural growth hormone production, sharpen libido, and improve sleep quality. Below is a naturopathic, Newport Beach–based guide to the peptides we use most often for men in Orange County and Rancho Cucamonga.

BPC-157 — Recovery and Gut Healing

BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound) is the go-to recovery peptide for men dealing with tendon injuries, joint pain, post-surgical healing, or GI inflammation. title: NAD IV Therapy: Benefits, What to Expect & How Often to Get It
excerpt: Learn what NAD IV therapy is, how it works, the benefits for energy, brain fog, anti-aging & addiction recovery, and how often you should get NAD+ infusions.
content:
If you've been researching ways to boost energy, sharpen mental clarity, or slow the aging process, you've likely come across NAD IV therapy. It's one of the most in-demand treatments in longevity and integrative medicine — and for good reason. NAD+ (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide) is a coenzyme found in every living cell, and its levels decline dramatically as we age.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine in Newport Beach and Rancho Cucamonga, we specialize in physician-supervised NAD IV therapy designed to restore cellular energy, support cognitive function, and help patients feel like themselves again. This guide covers everything you need to know about NAD IV infusions — what they are, how they work, what to expect, and whether they're right for you.

Looking for general IV therapy services? We offer a full range of IV nutrient drips including Myers' Cocktail, high-dose vitamin C, glutathione, and more.

What Is NAD IV Therapy?

NAD IV therapy is an intravenous infusion that delivers NAD+ directly into your bloodstream. Unlike oral supplements, which must pass through the digestive system and are absorbed at limited rates, IV administration provides 100% bioavailability — meaning your cells receive the full dose immediately.

NAD+ is essential for over 500 enzymatic reactions in the body. It fuels mitochondrial energy production, activates DNA repair enzymes (PARPs), switches on sirtuins (your "longevity genes"), and supports neurotransmitter synthesis. By age 50, most people have lost 40–50% of their NAD+ compared to their 20s. This decline is directly linked to fatigue, brain fog, metabolic slowdown, and accelerated cellular aging.

NAD IV therapy is not a vitamin drip — it's a cellular restoration treatment that works at the deepest level of biology to replenish what time and stress deplete.

NAD IV Therapy Benefits

Patients seek NAD IV therapy for a wide range of health goals. Here are the most well-documented benefits:

1. Energy Restoration at the Cellular Level

NAD+ is required for your mitochondria — the "power plants" inside your cells — to convert nutrients into ATP (cellular energy). When NAD+ is depleted, mitochondria struggle to produce energy, leading to persistent fatigue that sleep and caffeine can't fix. NAD IV therapy restores mitochondrial function from the inside out, often producing a noticeable energy boost within hours of the first infusion.

2. Cognitive Clarity & Brain Fog Relief

NAD+ supports the synthesis of dopamine, serotonin, and norepinephrine — neurotransmitters critical for focus, motivation, and mood stability. Patients frequently report reduced brain fog, improved memory, and sharper mental clarity after a series of NAD IV sessions. This makes NAD+ especially valuable for high-performers, executives, and anyone experiencing cognitive decline.

3. Anti-Aging & Longevity Support

NAD+ activates sirtuins, a family of proteins often called "longevity genes." Sirtuins regulate inflammation, DNA repair, metabolism, and cellular stress resistance. Higher NAD+ levels are associated with slower biological aging, better metabolic health, and reduced risk of age-related disease. NAD IV therapy is a cornerstone of comprehensive longevity medicine protocols.

4. Addiction Recovery Support

NAD+ helps rebalance neurotransmitter systems that are disrupted by substance use. IV NAD+ therapy has been used to support withdrawal symptom management and reduce cravings in patients recovering from alcohol, opioid, and stimulant dependency. It is not a standalone cure, but it can be a powerful adjunct to a structured recovery program.

5. Athletic Performance & Recovery

Athletes and active individuals use NAD IV therapy to accelerate muscle recovery, reduce oxidative stress, and improve endurance. By supporting mitochondrial efficiency and reducing inflammation at the cellular level, NAD+ helps the body repair faster and perform better.

6. Immune & Inflammatory Support

NAD+ plays a regulatory role in immune cell function and inflammatory signaling. Patients with autoimmune conditions, chronic infections, or long-haul symptoms often benefit from NAD IV therapy as part of a broader immune-modulating protocol.

What to Expect During a NAD IV Session

A typical NAD IV session at Reclaim Integrative Medicine lasts 2 to 4 hours, depending on the dose and your individual tolerance. Here's what the experience looks like:

  • Pre-session: You'll check in at our Newport Beach or Rancho Cucamonga clinic, where our team reviews your health history and confirms your protocol.
  • During the infusion: You'll relax in a comfortable treatment chair while the NAD+ solution is slowly infused through an IV line. We administer NAD+ gradually to minimize potential side effects like chest tightness, flushing, or nausea — all of which are temporary and resolve by adjusting the drip rate. If you're new to IV treatments, you can learn more about what to expect in our guide to IV therapy beyond the basics.
  • What to bring: Many patients read, work on a laptop, listen to podcasts, or simply rest during their session. We recommend bringing a book, headphones, or light work.
  • Post-session: Most patients feel energized and mentally clear within hours. Some report deeper, more restful sleep the night following their infusion.

NAD IV Protocol: How Often Should You Get It?

One of the most common questions we hear is: "How often should I do NAD IV therapy?" The answer depends on your goals, current health status, and budget.

Loading Phase

For patients with significant depletion — such as those with chronic fatigue, neurodegenerative concerns, or post-viral recovery — we typically recommend a loading phase of 2 to 4 sessions over 1 to 2 weeks. This builds up cellular NAD+ stores quickly and delivers the most dramatic initial results.

Maintenance Phase

After the loading phase, most patients transition to monthly maintenance infusions to sustain optimal NAD+ levels. Some high-performers or patients with ongoing health challenges choose bi-weekly sessions for a period of time.

Event-Based NAD IV

Some patients use NAD IV therapy on an as-needed basis — before a big project, after travel, during high-stress periods, or following illness. While less effective than a structured protocol, a single infusion can still deliver meaningful cognitive and energy benefits.

Is NAD IV Therapy Safe?

Yes — NAD IV therapy is generally very safe when administered by trained medical professionals in a clinical setting. NAD+ is a molecule your body already produces naturally; IV therapy simply restores levels that have declined.

The most common side effects are mild and temporary:

  • Warmth or flushing in the chest or face
  • Mild nausea
  • Lightheadedness
  • Fast heartbeat (transient)

These effects are almost always resolved by slowing the infusion rate. At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, our providers monitor every NAD IV session closely and adjust the protocol in real time for your comfort and safety.

NAD IV therapy may not be appropriate for patients with certain conditions. We conduct a full health screening before recommending treatment.

NAD IV vs. Oral NAD+ Supplements: Why IV Wins

Oral NAD+ supplements and precursors (like NMN and NR) have gained popularity, but they come with a major limitation: poor bioavailability. When swallowed, these compounds must survive stomach acid, pass through the intestinal wall, and survive first-pass liver metabolism before reaching your cells. Studies suggest only a small fraction of oral NAD+ precursors actually raise cellular NAD+ levels.

NAD IV therapy bypasses the digestive system entirely, delivering NAD+ directly into your bloodstream where it can be taken up by cells immediately. If you're looking for the fastest, most reliable way to raise NAD+ levels, IV administration is the gold standard.

That said, oral precursors and other NAD+ delivery methods — like subcutaneous injections and nasal spray — can be excellent for daily maintenance between IV sessions.

Who Is NAD IV Therapy Best For?

NAD IV therapy is ideal for patients who:

  • Experience persistent fatigue despite adequate sleep
  • Struggle with brain fog, poor concentration, or memory issues
  • Want to optimize longevity and slow biological aging
  • Are recovering from addiction and need neurotransmitter support
  • Are athletes or active individuals seeking faster recovery
  • Have chronic conditions involving mitochondrial dysfunction
  • Are going through perimenopause or menopause and notice energy/cognitive changes
  • Have recently recovered from a viral illness and want to rebuild cellular resilience

NAD IV Therapy in Newport Beach & Orange County

Reclaim Integrative Medicine offers physician-supervised NAD IV therapy at our clinics in Newport Beach and Rancho Cucamonga. Our protocols are individualized — we don't believe in one-size-fits-all medicine. Before your first infusion, we review your comprehensive health history, discuss your goals, and may recommend advanced lab testing to assess your current NAD+ status and mitochondrial function.

We combine NAD IV therapy with other advanced treatments like EBOO therapy, peptide therapy, hormone optimization, and targeted nutrition to create a comprehensive plan that addresses the root cause of your symptoms — not just the surface-level complaints.

Frequently Asked Questions About NAD IV Therapy

How long does a NAD IV session take?

Most NAD IV infusions take 2 to 4 hours. We administer NAD+ slowly to ensure comfort and safety. Faster infusions increase the risk of temporary side effects like chest tightness or flushing.

How soon will I feel the effects?

Many patients report increased energy and mental clarity within hours of their first session. However, the most significant benefits — improved sleep, sustained energy, sharper cognition — typically build over a series of treatments.

Does NAD IV therapy hurt?

The IV insertion feels like a small pinch, similar to any blood draw. Once the IV is in place, the infusion itself is painless. Some patients feel mild warmth or flushing during the session, which is normal and temporary.

Can I combine NAD IV with other IV therapies?

Yes. Many patients combine NAD+ with vitamin IV therapy, Plaquex, or glutathione for synergistic benefits. Our providers will design a protocol tailored to your needs. For mitochondrial and antioxidant stacking, we often pair NAD+ with alpha lipoic acid IV or IM injection therapy.

Is NAD IV therapy covered by insurance?

NAD IV therapy is typically considered an elective wellness or longevity treatment and is not covered by insurance. We provide itemized superbills that you can submit to your insurance for potential out-of-network reimbursement.

  • NAD+ Therapy: IV, Injectable & Nasal Spray Options — Compare NAD+ delivery methods and find the right option for your lifestyle.

Ready to Experience NAD IV Therapy?

If you're in Newport Beach, Orange County, or the Inland Empire and want to explore whether NAD IV therapy is right for you, we invite you to schedule a consultation with our team. We'll discuss your symptoms, review your health history, and create a personalized NAD+ protocol designed to help you reclaim your energy, clarity, and vitality.

NAD IV therapy is more than a wellness trend — it's a science-backed approach to cellular restoration that addresses the root cause of age-related decline. At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, we combine cutting-edge treatments with naturopathic philosophy to help you not just live longer, but live better.

title: Low Dose Naltrexone (LDN): Uses, Benefits & What to Expect
excerpt: Low Dose Naltrexone (LDN) is used for autoimmune conditions, Hashimoto's, fibromyalgia, chronic pain, long COVID and inflammation. Learn how LDN works, dosing, side effects, and how to get a prescription.
content:
Low Dose Naltrexone (LDN) is one of the most underused tools in integrative medicine. Originally developed at a much higher dose for opioid and alcohol use disorders, naltrexone behaves very differently at the low doses used in LDN protocols (typically 1.5–4.5 mg at bedtime). At these doses, it modulates the immune system, calms chronic inflammation, and supports the body's own production of endorphins — making it useful for a wide range of autoimmune, pain, and inflammatory conditions.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, we prescribe LDN for patients in Newport Beach, Orange County, and Rancho Cucamonga as part of a comprehensive, root-cause naturopathic plan.

What Is Low Dose Naltrexone?

Naltrexone is a long-acting opioid receptor antagonist. At the standard 50 mg dose it fully blocks opioid receptors. At 1.5–4.5 mg, it briefly and partially blocks those same receptors for just a few hours overnight. That short blockade triggers a rebound increase in endorphins and enkephalins — your body's natural anti-inflammatory and pain-modulating chemicals — and downregulates inflammatory signaling in immune cells (especially microglia in the brain).

The result is a medication that is non-narcotic, non-habit-forming, inexpensive, and remarkably well-tolerated for most patients.

What Is LDN Used For?

LDN is prescribed off-label for conditions where chronic immune activation and inflammation are key drivers. The most common uses include:

  • Autoimmune disease — Hashimoto's thyroiditis, rheumatoid arthritis, psoriasis, lupus, Crohn's, ulcerative colitis, and multiple sclerosis
  • Chronic pain & fibromyalgia — particularly central sensitization pain that hasn't responded to standard treatment
  • Hashimoto's & thyroid autoimmunity — often paired with our thyroid care protocols
  • Long COVID and post-viral syndromes — especially when fatigue and brain fog dominate
  • Mast cell activation and chronic allergic inflammation
  • Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) — Crohn's and UC have the strongest research base
  • Mood symptoms tied to inflammation — depression and anxiety with an inflammatory component
  • Chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS) — see our notes on managing chronic fatigue

How Does Low Dose Naltrexone Work?

LDN works through three main mechanisms:

  1. Endorphin upregulation. The brief opioid blockade overnight signals the body to make more endogenous opioids (endorphins/enkephalins), which have direct anti-inflammatory and immune-balancing effects.
  2. Microglial modulation. LDN binds to toll-like receptor 4 (TLR4) on microglia — the immune cells of the central nervous system — quieting the neuroinflammation that drives fatigue, brain fog, and central pain.
  3. Immune regulation. LDN shifts the immune system toward a more balanced Th1/Th2/Th17 profile and reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines like TNF-alpha and IL-6.

Why Take LDN at Night?

LDN is dosed at bedtime because the endorphin rebound peaks during the early morning hours when your body's natural opioid production is highest. Dosing at night maximizes that rebound and minimizes daytime side effects. If LDN causes vivid dreams or sleep disruption (the most common early side effect), some patients move the dose to the morning instead — both work.

LDN Dosing Protocol

LDN must be obtained from a compounding pharmacy because the commercial 50 mg tablet cannot be accurately split to LDN doses. We typically start patients at:

  • Week 1–2: 1.5 mg at bedtime
  • Week 3–4: 3.0 mg at bedtime
  • Week 5+: 4.5 mg at bedtime (target dose for most patients)

Some patients with autonomic sensitivity, fibromyalgia, or long COVID do better starting even lower (0.5 mg liquid) and titrating slowly. Others find their "sweet spot" at 3.0 mg and don't need to climb to 4.5. We adjust based on symptom response, not a fixed protocol.

LDN Side Effects

LDN is well-tolerated by the vast majority of patients. The most common side effects are mild and usually resolve within 1–2 weeks:

  • Vivid dreams or disrupted sleep (most common — usually transient)
  • Headache
  • Mild nausea or GI upset
  • Temporary fatigue during titration

LDN is not appropriate if you are taking opioid pain medication, suboxone, or tramadol — the partial opioid blockade can precipitate withdrawal. It also requires careful coordination with thyroid medication, since immune calming can change your levothyroxine requirement (often down).

What to Avoid When Taking Low Dose Naltrexone

  • Opioid medications — including tramadol, codeine, and suboxone
  • Opioid-containing cough syrups — read labels carefully
  • Immunosuppressants only with physician oversight — LDN can amplify immune effects
  • Stopping abruptly without monitoring thyroid labs if you have Hashimoto's

How Long Until LDN Works?

Most patients feel a difference in 4–8 weeks, with full effects often emerging by 3–6 months. Pain and sleep tend to improve first; autoimmune markers (TPO, CRP, etc.) often improve over 3–12 months. LDN is a long-game medication — patience and consistent dosing matter.

How to Get LDN in Newport Beach & Rancho Cucamonga

LDN is a prescription medication, so it requires a consultation with a licensed prescriber. At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, our naturopathic doctors evaluate whether LDN is right for you alongside comprehensive labs, a thorough history, and a plan for the underlying root causes — whether that's autoimmune triggers, gut health, hormone imbalance, or chronic infection.

LDN often pairs well with other tools we use, including peptide therapy, IV therapy, and EBOO for inflammatory conditions.

Is LDN Right for You?

If you have an autoimmune condition, chronic pain, fibromyalgia, long COVID, or unexplained inflammation that hasn't responded to conventional care, LDN is worth a conversation. Patients can schedule a new patient consultation at our Newport Beach clinic or our Rancho Cucamonga clinic in the Inland Empire to discuss LDN as part of a personalized naturopathic plan.

title: The Labs I Wish Every Mom Knew to Ask For (At Every Stage of Life)
excerpt: Dr. Andrea Colon was featured in Hello Mamas sharing the essential lab tests every mom should request — from fertility and pregnancy through postpartum and beyond.
content:
Dr. Andrea Colon, NMD, was recently featured in Hello Mamas — a leading parenting and motherhood platform — sharing the lab tests she believes every mom should know to ask for at every stage of life.

From trying to conceive to navigating postpartum recovery and beyond, the right labs can reveal hidden imbalances that standard check-ups often miss. Here's a summary of Dr. Colon's recommendations.

Fertility & Pre-Conception

Before or while trying to conceive, Dr. Colon recommends going beyond a basic CBC and metabolic panel. Key labs include:

  • Full thyroid panel (TSH, Free T3, Free T4, thyroid antibodies) — thyroid dysfunction is one of the most common yet under-diagnosed causes of infertility and miscarriage
  • Vitamin D — essential for hormone production and immune regulation
  • Iron & ferritin — low iron stores can affect energy, ovulation, and pregnancy outcomes
  • AMH (Anti-Müllerian Hormone) — provides insight into ovarian reserve
  • Full hormone panel — estrogen, progesterone, testosterone, DHEA-S

During Pregnancy

Pregnancy places enormous demands on the body. Dr. Colon suggests monitoring:

  • Thyroid function each trimester — needs change significantly during pregnancy
  • Iron and ferritin — demands increase dramatically
  • Vitamin D and B12 — critical for fetal development
  • Blood sugar & insulin — not just glucose, but fasting insulin for early detection of metabolic shifts
  • Inflammation markers (hs-CRP) — elevated inflammation may signal complications

Postpartum Recovery

The postpartum period — often called the "fourth trimester" — is when many women experience profound hormonal shifts. Dr. Colon emphasizes:

  • Thyroid panel — postpartum thyroiditis is extremely common and often mistaken for depression or "normal" exhaustion
  • Iron & ferritin — especially after blood loss during delivery
  • Cortisol & DHEA — adrenal health is critical during sleep-deprived months
  • Full hormone panel — progesterone and estrogen fluctuations drive many postpartum symptoms
  • Nutrient levels (magnesium, zinc, B vitamins) — often depleted from pregnancy and breastfeeding

Perimenopause & Beyond (35+)

Many women don't realize perimenopause can begin in the mid-30s. Labs Dr. Colon recommends at this stage:

  • FSH & estradiol — early indicators of hormonal transition
  • Full thyroid panel — thyroid issues increase with age
  • Fasting insulin & HbA1c — metabolic health becomes harder to maintain
  • Inflammatory markers & homocysteine — cardiovascular risk increases after menopause
  • Vitamin D, B12, magnesium — foundational nutrients for bone health and mood

Why Standard Panels Aren't Enough

Dr. Colon explains that conventional medicine often runs a limited panel — TSH only for thyroid, glucose only for blood sugar, and skips hormone testing entirely unless there's a major complaint. This reactive approach means women suffer with symptoms for years before getting answers.

A comprehensive lab approach looks at the full picture — identifying patterns and catching imbalances before they become chronic conditions.

title: NAD+ Therapy: IV, Injectable & Nasal Spray Options
excerpt: Compare NAD+ delivery methods — IV drip for maximum restoration, subcutaneous injections for convenience, and nasal spray for cognitive focus. Find the right option for you.
content:
NAD+ (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide) has become one of the most talked-about molecules in longevity and integrative medicine — and for good reason. It's essential for energy production, DNA repair, immune function, and cellular communication. The challenge? NAD+ levels decline significantly as we age, contributing to fatigue, brain fog, and accelerated aging.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, we offer multiple ways to restore your NAD+ levels. Each delivery method has distinct advantages depending on your health goals, schedule, and preferences.

Why NAD+ Matters

NAD+ is a coenzyme found in every living cell. It fuels over 500 enzymatic reactions in your body, including:

  • Mitochondrial energy production — NAD+ is required for your cells to convert food into ATP (energy)
  • DNA repair — activates PARP enzymes that fix damaged DNA strands
  • Sirtuin activation — sirtuins are your "longevity genes" that regulate aging, inflammation, and metabolism
  • Neurotransmitter synthesis — supports production of dopamine, serotonin, and norepinephrine
  • Circadian rhythm regulation — helps maintain healthy sleep-wake cycles

By age 50, most people have lost 40-50% of their NAD+ compared to their 20s. This decline is linked to fatigue, cognitive decline, metabolic dysfunction, and increased disease risk.

NAD+ IV Therapy

IV NAD+ therapy delivers the coenzyme directly into your bloodstream for 100% bioavailability. This is our most potent NAD+ option.

Best For

  • Significant energy restoration and chronic fatigue
  • Cognitive enhancement and brain fog recovery
  • Anti-aging and longevity protocols
  • Addiction recovery support
  • Post-illness or post-surgical recovery

What to Expect

NAD+ IV sessions typically last 2-4 hours depending on the dose. The infusion is administered slowly to minimize potential side effects like chest tightness or flushing, which are temporary and resolve by adjusting the drip rate. Most patients describe feeling a noticeable boost in energy and mental clarity within hours of their infusion.

Recommended Protocol

For optimal results, we typically recommend a loading phase of 2-4 sessions over 1-2 weeks, followed by monthly maintenance infusions. Patients with more significant depletion — such as those with chronic fatigue or neurodegenerative concerns — may benefit from more frequent sessions initially.

NAD+ Injectable Peptide

For patients who want consistent NAD+ support without the time commitment of an IV, subcutaneous NAD+ injections offer a convenient alternative.

Best For

  • Ongoing NAD+ maintenance between IV sessions
  • Busy patients who can't commit to lengthy IV appointments
  • Gradual, steady NAD+ repletion
  • Patients already comfortable with self-injection (similar to peptide therapy)

What to Expect

NAD+ injections are quick subcutaneous shots — similar to insulin injections — that can be self-administered at home after proper training. The dose is lower per session than IV but accumulates with consistent use. Most patients inject 3-5 times per week for best results.

Advantages

  • Takes less than 1 minute to administer
  • Can be done at home on your own schedule
  • More affordable per session than IV
  • Pairs well with other peptide protocols like the Wolverine Stack or tesamorelin

NAD+ Nasal Spray

The newest and most convenient NAD+ delivery method, nasal spray offers rapid absorption through the nasal mucosa with direct access to the brain.

Best For

  • Cognitive focus and mental clarity — the nasal route has the most direct pathway to the brain
  • Patients who prefer needle-free options
  • Daily maintenance and prevention
  • Travel-friendly NAD+ support

What to Expect

NAD+ nasal spray is used 1-2 times daily with a few sprays per dose. Many patients notice improved focus and mental sharpness within 15-30 minutes of use. It's the most convenient option for daily NAD+ support.

Advantages

  • No needles required
  • Rapid onset — especially for cognitive benefits
  • Easy to use anywhere, anytime
  • Can be combined with IV or injectable protocols for layered support

Which NAD+ Option Should You Choose?

The best approach depends on your goals and lifestyle:

  • Maximum restoration → Start with IV loading, then maintain with injections or nasal spray
  • Convenience first → Nasal spray for daily use with occasional IV boosts
  • Already doing peptides → Add injectable NAD+ to your existing protocol
  • Comprehensive longevity plan → Combine all three in a phased approach

Many of our patients use a combination strategy — an IV session monthly for deep repletion, with injectable or nasal spray NAD+ in between for sustained levels.

NAD+ as Part of Your Longevity Plan

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, NAD+ therapy is one piece of a comprehensive longevity medicine approach. NAD+ IV is available through our IV therapy program, and injectable NAD+ is part of our peptide therapy offerings. We combine NAD+ with hormone optimization, mitochondrial support, targeted nutrition, and advanced lab testing to help you not just live longer — but live better.

If you're in Newport Beach, Orange County, or the Inland Empire and want to explore which NAD+ option is right for you, schedule a consultation with our team.

title: The Wolverine Stack: BPC-157 + TB-500 for Ultimate Healing
excerpt: Discover why the Wolverine Stack — combining BPC-157 and TB-500 — is one of the most powerful healing protocols in integrative medicine for injuries, recovery, and chronic pain.
content:
If you've spent any time researching peptides for healing and recovery, you've likely come across the term "Wolverine Stack" — named after the Marvel character known for his superhuman ability to heal. While we can't promise comic-book-level regeneration, the combination of BPC-157 and TB-500 is one of the most powerful healing protocols available in integrative medicine today.

What Is TB-500?

TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4) is a naturally occurring peptide found in virtually all human and animal cells. It plays a critical role in tissue repair, cell migration, and the formation of new blood vessels — a process called angiogenesis.

TB-500's healing properties include:

  • Accelerated wound healing — promotes faster recovery from injuries, surgeries, and tissue damage
  • Reduced inflammation — modulates inflammatory pathways without suppressing immune function
  • New blood vessel formation — improves blood supply to damaged tissues for faster repair
  • Increased flexibility — promotes tissue remodeling and reduces scar tissue formation
  • Cardiac protection — research shows benefits for heart tissue repair and function
  • Hair regrowth potential — stimulates hair follicle stem cells

BPC-157: The Perfect Partner

We covered BPC-157 in our peptide therapy overview, but here's why it pairs so well with TB-500:

  • BPC-157 heals from the inside out — it's particularly effective for gut healing, tendon/ligament repair, and protecting against NSAID damage
  • TB-500 heals from the outside in — it excels at systemic tissue repair, reducing inflammation, and building new vasculature
  • Together, they cover every angle — different mechanisms of action that complement and amplify each other

Why It's Called the "Wolverine Stack"

The nickname comes from the remarkable healing acceleration patients experience when combining both peptides. Common results include:

  • 50-70% faster recovery from soft tissue injuries compared to rest alone
  • Significant reduction in chronic pain from old injuries
  • Improved joint mobility and reduced stiffness
  • Better surgical recovery outcomes
  • Resolution of nagging injuries that haven't responded to other treatments

Who Benefits from the Wolverine Stack?

This protocol is ideal for:

  • Athletes and active individuals — faster recovery from training, sprains, strains, and overuse injuries
  • Post-surgical patients — accelerated tissue repair and reduced scar formation
  • Chronic pain sufferers — especially tendinitis, plantar fasciitis, rotator cuff issues, and joint pain
  • Gut health patients — BPC-157 is exceptional for healing digestive conditions like leaky gut, gastritis, and inflammatory bowel issues
  • Anyone with slow-healing injuries — old injuries that never fully resolved
  • Hair loss patients — TB-500's stem cell activation may support hair regrowth

How We Use the Wolverine Stack

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, we customize the Wolverine Stack protocol based on your specific needs:

  • Dosing — individualized based on injury severity, body weight, and treatment goals
  • Duration — typically 6-12 weeks, with some patients benefiting from maintenance dosing
  • Administration — subcutaneous injections, usually once daily or several times per week
  • Combination therapy — we often pair the Wolverine Stack with IV therapy and targeted nutrition for maximum healing support

TB-500 as a Standalone

While the Wolverine Stack is our most popular healing protocol, TB-500 can also be used on its own for patients who:

  • Need systemic anti-inflammatory support
  • Are focused primarily on cardiovascular or cardiac health
  • Want to support tissue flexibility and reduce fibrosis
  • Are exploring peptides for hair restoration

Safety and Considerations

Both BPC-157 and TB-500 have excellent safety profiles based on available research. Side effects are rare and typically limited to mild injection site reactions. As with all peptide therapies, proper sourcing and medical supervision are essential — which is why we only use pharmaceutical-grade peptides and monitor all patients closely.

title: Tesamorelin: The Peptide That Targets Stubborn Belly Fat
excerpt: Learn how tesamorelin stimulates natural growth hormone production to reduce visceral fat, improve body composition, and support longevity.
content:
Stubborn belly fat isn't just a cosmetic concern — it's a health risk. Visceral fat, the deep abdominal fat that wraps around your organs, is metabolically active tissue that drives inflammation, insulin resistance, and cardiovascular disease. At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, tesamorelin is one of our most effective tools for targeting this dangerous fat.

What Is Tesamorelin?

Tesamorelin is a growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) analog — meaning it stimulates your pituitary gland to produce and release more of your own growth hormone naturally. Unlike direct growth hormone injections, tesamorelin works through your body's own feedback systems, which means a more physiological and safer response.

Originally FDA-approved for reducing lipodystrophy (abnormal fat accumulation), tesamorelin has gained significant attention in integrative and longevity medicine for its broader metabolic benefits.

How Tesamorelin Works

As we age, growth hormone production declines — typically by about 14% per decade after age 30. This decline contributes to increased body fat (especially visceral fat), decreased muscle mass, reduced energy, and accelerated aging. Tesamorelin helps reverse this trend by:

  • Stimulating natural GH release — pulsatile secretion that mimics your body's youthful pattern
  • Targeting visceral adipose tissue — clinical studies show significant reductions in trunk fat
  • Improving lipid profiles — reduces triglycerides and may improve cholesterol ratios
  • Preserving lean muscle mass — supports body recomposition, not just weight loss
  • Supporting cognitive function — growth hormone plays a role in memory and mental clarity

Clinical Evidence

Tesamorelin has robust clinical data behind it. Key study findings include:

  • Up to 18% reduction in visceral fat over 26 weeks of treatment
  • Significant improvements in triglyceride levels
  • No significant impact on glucose metabolism at standard doses
  • Benefits maintained with continued use

Tesamorelin vs. Other Growth Hormone Peptides

You may have heard of other GH-stimulating peptides like CJC-1295/Ipamorelin. Here's how tesamorelin compares:

  • More targeted fat loss — tesamorelin has stronger evidence specifically for visceral fat reduction
  • FDA background — tesamorelin has been through rigorous clinical trials
  • Complementary options — some patients benefit from combining tesamorelin with other peptides as part of a comprehensive longevity medicine program

Who Is a Good Candidate?

Tesamorelin may be right for you if you're experiencing:

  • Stubborn abdominal fat that won't respond to diet and exercise
  • Age-related decline in muscle mass and energy
  • Elevated triglycerides or metabolic syndrome markers
  • Interest in evidence-based anti-aging and longevity strategies
  • Weight loss plateaus despite being on a medical weight loss program

Tesamorelin pairs well with GLP medications, hormone optimization, and targeted nutrition — all of which we offer at our practice.

What to Expect

Tesamorelin is administered as a daily subcutaneous injection, typically before bedtime to align with your body's natural growth hormone release pattern. Most patients begin seeing measurable body composition changes within 6-8 weeks, with optimal results at 12-26 weeks.

Side effects are generally mild and may include injection site reactions, joint stiffness, or temporary water retention as GH levels optimize.

Beyond Fat Loss: The Longevity Connection

Reducing visceral fat isn't just about aesthetics — it's one of the most impactful things you can do for your long-term health. Lower visceral fat means reduced cardiovascular risk, improved insulin sensitivity, less systemic inflammation, and potentially a longer, healthier life.

Tesamorelin is available as part of our peptide therapy services. It pairs well with our medical weight loss program for patients looking for a comprehensive approach. If you're in Newport Beach, Orange County, or the Inland Empire, our team is ready to help.

title: Glow & Klow: The Peptide Duo for Radiant Skin & Calm

content:

What Is Glow?

Glow is a peptide blend designed to enhance skin health, elasticity, and radiance at the cellular level. Unlike topical skincare that works on the surface, Glow targets the biological processes that determine how your skin looks and ages.

Benefits of Glow peptide therapy include:

  • Increased collagen production — the structural protein that keeps skin firm and youthful
  • Improved skin elasticity — reducing fine lines and wrinkles from within
  • Enhanced skin hydration — better moisture retention at the cellular level

What Is Klow?

Klow works by modulating key pathways involved in your body's stress response:

  • Cortisol regulation — helps normalize the HPA axis when chronic stress has thrown it out of balance
  • Improved sleep quality — supports deeper, more restorative sleep cycles
  • Reduced anxiety — calms the nervous system without cognitive dulling

Patients who use both often report:
