title: Medical Weight Loss with Semaglutide & Tirzepatide
excerpt: Everything you need to know about GLP medications for weight loss — how they work, what to expect, and why a naturopathic approach delivers better, lasting results.
content:
If you've been struggling with weight loss despite eating well and exercising, you're not alone — and it's not a willpower problem. Hormones, metabolism, and biology play a far bigger role than most people realize. At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, our medical supervised weight loss program combines GLP medications like semaglutide and tirzepatide with the naturopathic approach your body needs for lasting results.

What Are GLP Medications?

GLP medications — including semaglutide (the active ingredient in Ozempic® and Wegovy®) and tirzepatide (the active ingredient in Mounjaro® and Zepbound®) — are injectable medications that mimic hormones your body naturally produces to regulate appetite, blood sugar, and metabolism.

These medications work through multiple mechanisms:

  • Appetite reduction — they act on brain receptors that control hunger and satiety, so you feel genuinely satisfied with less food
  • Slowed gastric emptying — food stays in your stomach longer, keeping you full between meals
  • Improved insulin sensitivity — better blood sugar regulation reduces fat storage
  • Reduced cravings — many patients report a significant decrease in food noise and compulsive eating

Semaglutide vs. Tirzepatide: What's the Difference?

Both medications are highly effective, but they work slightly differently:

Semaglutide

A GLP receptor agonist that has been extensively studied for both diabetes management and weight loss. Clinical trials showed an average weight loss of 15-17% of body weight over 68 weeks. Semaglutide is administered as a once-weekly injection with gradual dose titration.

Tirzepatide

A dual GLP and GIP receptor agonist — meaning it targets two metabolic pathways instead of one. Clinical trials demonstrated even more impressive results, with participants losing up to 22% of body weight. Tirzepatide also shows strong benefits for insulin resistance and metabolic health.

Why a Naturopathic Approach to GLP Therapy Matters

Here's what sets our program apart from simply getting a prescription: we treat the whole picture, not just the number on the scale.

  • Comprehensive lab work — we test thyroid function, hormones, insulin, inflammatory markers, and nutrient levels before starting. Undiagnosed thyroid dysfunction or hormonal imbalances can sabotage weight loss efforts
  • Nutritional guidance — GLP medications reduce appetite, but what you eat still matters. We ensure you're getting adequate protein and nutrients to preserve muscle mass
  • Gut health support — GI side effects are the most common complaint with GLP medications. Our naturopathic protocols help minimize nausea, constipation, and digestive discomfort
  • Hormone optimization — weight loss can be significantly harder with suboptimal thyroid, cortisol, or sex hormone levels. We address these concurrently
  • Metabolic support — targeted supplementation and B12 injections to support energy and metabolism during weight loss
  • Exit strategy — we plan for what happens when you reach your goal, helping you transition off medication while maintaining results

What to Expect on GLP Therapy

Most patients begin noticing appetite changes within the first 1-2 weeks. Meaningful weight loss typically becomes visible by weeks 4-8 as the dose is gradually increased to your therapeutic level.

A typical timeline looks like:

  • Weeks 1-4: Starting dose, appetite begins to decrease, minimal weight change
  • Weeks 4-12: Dose titration, noticeable weight loss of 1-2 lbs per week
  • Months 3-6: Steady weight loss, improved energy, better lab markers
  • Months 6-12+: Approaching goal weight, focus shifts to maintenance and metabolic health

Managing Side Effects Naturally

The most common side effects of GLP medications are gastrointestinal — nausea, constipation, and occasionally diarrhea. Our naturopathic toolkit is uniquely suited to manage these:

  • Ginger and digestive enzymes for nausea
  • Magnesium and fiber optimization for constipation
  • Probiotics and gut-healing nutrients for overall GI comfort
  • Slow, individualized dose titration — we increase doses based on your tolerance, not a rigid schedule

Who Is a Good Candidate?

GLP medications may be appropriate if you:

  • Have a BMI of 27+ with metabolic concerns, or BMI of 30+
  • Have struggled with weight loss despite genuine diet and exercise efforts
  • Have insulin resistance, pre-diabetes, or type 2 diabetes
  • Are dealing with weight gain related to hormonal changes (perimenopause, PCOS, thyroid issues)
  • Want a medically supervised, evidence-based approach

Start Your Weight Loss Journey in Orange County

If you're in Newport Beach, Orange County, or the Inland Empire and ready to explore whether semaglutide or tirzepatide is right for you, learn more about our medical weight loss program. You may also benefit from tesamorelin peptide therapy for targeting stubborn belly fat. At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, we help you lose weight and get healthier in the process.

title: Peptide Therapy: The Future of Regenerative Medicine
excerpt: Learn how peptide therapy with BPC-157, Thymosin Alpha-1, and growth hormone peptides can accelerate healing, boost immunity, and optimize your health.
content:
Peptide therapy is one of the most exciting developments in regenerative and integrative medicine — and it's rapidly becoming a cornerstone of how we help patients heal, recover, and optimize at Reclaim Integrative Medicine.

What Are Peptides?

Peptides are short chains of amino acids — essentially small proteins — that act as signaling molecules in your body. They tell your cells what to do: repair tissue, reduce inflammation, produce growth hormone, improve gut healing, and much more.

Your body naturally produces thousands of peptides, but production declines with age, stress, and chronic illness. Therapeutic peptides are bioidentical versions designed to restore or amplify these natural processes.

Key Peptides We Use in Practice

BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound)

Originally derived from a protective protein in gastric juice, BPC-157 is one of the most well-researched healing peptides available. It supports:

  • Gut healing — particularly effective for leaky gut, IBS, and inflammatory bowel conditions
  • Tissue repair — accelerates healing of tendons, ligaments, muscles, and joints
  • Neuroprotection — supports brain health and may aid in recovery from concussions
  • Reducing inflammation — systemic anti-inflammatory effects

Thymosin Alpha-1

A powerful immune-modulating peptide that enhances your body's ability to fight infections and regulate immune function. Especially valuable for patients with:

  • Chronic viral infections (EBV, Lyme co-infections)
  • Autoimmune conditions
  • Mold exposure and mycotoxin illness
  • Weakened immune function

CJC-1295 / Ipamorelin

This combination stimulates your body's natural growth hormone production — without the risks of direct growth hormone injection. Benefits include:

  • Improved body composition — increased lean muscle, decreased body fat
  • Better sleep quality — growth hormone is primarily released during deep sleep
  • Enhanced recovery — faster healing from workouts and injuries
  • Anti-aging effects — improved skin elasticity, energy, and vitality

PT-141 (Bremelanotide)

A peptide that works through the central nervous system to improve sexual desire and function in both men and women — a different mechanism than medications like Viagra that only address blood flow.

KPV

An anti-inflammatory peptide derived from alpha-MSH that is particularly effective for gut inflammation, skin conditions, and systemic inflammatory states.

How Peptide Therapy Works

Peptides can be administered via subcutaneous injection, oral capsules, nasal sprays, or topical creams depending on the specific peptide and your treatment goals. Most injectable peptides involve a simple, painless self-injection with a tiny insulin needle — similar to what diabetic patients use daily.

Treatment protocols typically run 4-12 weeks depending on the condition being treated, with some patients benefiting from ongoing or cyclical use.

Who Benefits from Peptide Therapy?

Peptides are remarkably versatile. We commonly recommend them for patients dealing with:

  • Chronic fatigue and low energy
  • Digestive issues — especially leaky gut and SIBO recovery
  • Sports injuries and slow-healing tissues
  • Age-related decline in muscle mass, sleep quality, or libido
  • Immune dysfunction and frequent illness
  • Post-surgical healing support
  • Weight management plateaus

Peptides vs. Traditional Medications

One of the most appealing aspects of peptide therapy is that peptides work with your body's natural biology rather than overriding it. They stimulate your own healing mechanisms, which typically means:

  • Fewer side effects than pharmaceutical alternatives
  • Synergy with other naturopathic treatments
  • Support for long-term healing rather than just symptom suppression

Is Peptide Therapy Right for You?

Peptide therapy works best when integrated into a comprehensive treatment plan guided by lab work and clinical assessment. During your consultation, we'll evaluate whether peptides are appropriate for your specific health goals and which protocols would be most effective.

title: Understanding Thyroid Health: Signs You Shouldn't Ignore
excerpt: Learn about the subtle signs of thyroid dysfunction and why early detection is crucial for your overall health and wellbeing.
content:
Your thyroid — a small, butterfly-shaped gland at the base of your neck — controls nearly every metabolic process in your body. When it's not functioning optimally, the symptoms can be wide-ranging and easily dismissed. At Reclaim Integrative Medicine in Orange County, we see patients every week who have been struggling with undiagnosed or under-treated thyroid conditions for years.

Why Standard Thyroid Testing Falls Short

Most conventional doctors only test TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone). While TSH is an important marker, it doesn't tell the full story. A comprehensive thyroid panel should include:

  • Free T3 and Free T4 — the active thyroid hormones your cells actually use
  • Reverse T3 — which can block thyroid hormone at the cellular level
  • Thyroid antibodies (TPO & TgAb) — to screen for autoimmune thyroid conditions like Hashimoto's

At our practice, comprehensive lab testing is the foundation of every treatment plan. We believe you can't fix what you can't see.

Signs Your Thyroid May Need Attention

Thyroid dysfunction doesn't always present as a textbook case. Here are subtle signs many patients overlook:

  • Persistent fatigue despite adequate sleep
  • Unexplained weight gain or difficulty losing weight
  • Hair thinning or loss, especially at the outer eyebrows
  • Brain fog, poor concentration, or memory issues
  • Feeling cold when others are comfortable
  • Constipation or sluggish digestion
  • Dry skin, brittle nails
  • Mood changes — anxiety, depression, or irritability
  • Irregular or heavy menstrual cycles

The Hashimoto's Connection

Hashimoto's thyroiditis is the most common cause of hypothyroidism in the United States, and it's an autoimmune condition — meaning your immune system is attacking your own thyroid. Many patients with Hashimoto's are told their labs are "normal" because their TSH hasn't risen high enough to warrant medication, even though antibodies are actively destroying the gland.

A naturopathic approach to Hashimoto's goes beyond thyroid hormone replacement. We address the root causes driving the autoimmune response — which often include gut permeability, food sensitivities, chronic infections, and nutrient deficiencies.

Our Naturopathic Approach to Thyroid Health

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, our thyroid health program includes:

  • Full thyroid panel with antibody testing
  • Assessment of nutrient cofactors (selenium, zinc, iron, vitamin D, iodine)
  • Gut health evaluation — the gut-thyroid axis is real
  • Adrenal function testing — chronic stress directly suppresses thyroid conversion
  • Personalized treatment plans combining lifestyle, nutrition, and targeted supplementation
  • Medication management when needed, including natural desiccated thyroid options

When to See a Naturopathic Doctor for Your Thyroid

If you've been told your labs are "normal" but you still don't feel right, it may be time for a deeper look. Learn more about our thyroid health services and comprehensive lab testing. If you're in Newport Beach, Orange County, or the Inland Empire, we'd love to help you get to the root of your thyroid concerns.

title: The Gut-Brain Connection: How Digestive Health Affects Your Mood
excerpt: Discover the fascinating link between your digestive system and mental health, and what you can do to support both.
content:
Have you ever had a "gut feeling"? That's not just a metaphor. Your gastrointestinal tract contains over 500 million neurons — often called your "second brain" — and produces approximately 95% of your body's serotonin, the neurotransmitter most closely linked to mood regulation.

What Is the Gut-Brain Axis?

The gut-brain axis is a bidirectional communication network between your central nervous system and your enteric nervous system. This connection runs through the vagus nerve, immune signaling molecules, and the metabolites produced by your gut bacteria.

When your gut microbiome is out of balance — a condition called dysbiosis — it can directly contribute to:

  • Anxiety and panic attacks
  • Depression and low motivation
  • Brain fog and poor concentration
  • Insomnia and disrupted sleep
  • Chronic inflammation throughout the body

Common Gut Issues We See in Practice

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, our digestive health program addresses conditions that conventional medicine often overlooks or undertreats:

  • SIBO (Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth) — one of the most common causes of bloating, gas, and IBS symptoms
  • Leaky gut (intestinal permeability) — allows toxins and undigested food particles into the bloodstream, triggering immune reactions
  • Parasitic infections — more common than you'd think, especially after travel
  • Candida overgrowth — yeast imbalances that affect energy, mood, and digestion
  • Food sensitivities — distinct from allergies, these delayed reactions can cause chronic symptoms

How We Investigate Gut Health

We use advanced functional testing that goes far beyond a standard stool test:

  • Comprehensive stool analysis with PCR technology to identify bacteria, yeast, parasites, and digestive markers
  • SIBO breath testing to detect hydrogen and methane-producing bacteria
  • Food sensitivity panels to identify trigger foods
  • Organic acids testing for metabolic and microbial markers

Healing the Gut Naturally

Our naturopathic approach follows a proven framework: Remove, Replace, Reinoculate, Repair, and Rebalance. This means eliminating pathogens and trigger foods, restoring digestive enzymes and stomach acid, reintroducing beneficial bacteria, repairing the gut lining, and addressing lifestyle factors like stress and sleep.

Many of our patients notice significant improvements in both their digestive symptoms and their mood within the first few weeks of treatment. When you fix the gut, the brain often follows.

Is Your Gut Affecting Your Mental Health?

If you're experiencing digestive issues alongside anxiety, depression, or mood changes, the connection may not be coincidental. A comprehensive evaluation can uncover whether your gut is at the root of how you're feeling.

Explore our digestive health services to learn how we can help restore your gut-brain connection and improve both your digestive and mental health.

title: Bioidentical Hormones: What You Need to Know
excerpt: An in-depth look at bioidentical hormone replacement therapy, its benefits, and who might be a good candidate.
content:
Hormonal imbalances don't just affect your reproductive health — they impact your energy, mood, sleep, weight, cognitive function, and even your cardiovascular health. At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, bioidentical hormone replacement therapy (BHRT) is one of our most sought-after services, and for good reason.

What Are Bioidentical Hormones?

Bioidentical hormones are molecularly identical to the hormones your body naturally produces. Unlike synthetic hormones (such as Premarin or Provera), bioidentical hormones fit your body's hormone receptors perfectly, which typically means fewer side effects and better outcomes.

Common bioidentical hormones we prescribe include:

  • Estradiol (E2) — the primary estrogen
  • Progesterone — crucial for sleep, mood, and uterine health
  • Testosterone — important for both men and women
  • DHEA — an adrenal hormone precursor

Our Hormone Therapy Options

We offer multiple hormone replacement approaches because every patient is different:

Biote Hormone Pellets

Tiny pellets inserted under the skin that deliver a consistent, steady dose of hormones over 3-6 months. Pellet therapy eliminates the daily hassle of creams or pills and avoids the hormonal peaks and valleys that other delivery methods can cause.

Wiley Protocol

A rhythmic dosing method that mimics your body's natural hormone cycles. Rather than giving the same dose every day, the Wiley Protocol follows the pattern your body would produce if your hormones were functioning optimally — particularly beneficial for women in perimenopause.

Rhythmic Hormone Protocol

Our customized approach to cyclical hormone dosing, tailored to your lab results, symptoms, and health history.

Who Benefits from BHRT?

Hormone therapy isn't just for menopause. We frequently help patients experiencing:

  • Perimenopause — symptoms can start in your late 30s or early 40s
  • PCOS — polycystic ovary syndrome involves complex hormonal imbalances
  • Low testosterone in men — fatigue, decreased libido, muscle loss, and mood changes
  • Post-surgical menopause — after hysterectomy or oophorectomy
  • Adrenal fatigue — chronic stress depletes hormone precursors
  • Age-related decline — hormone production naturally decreases over time

TRT for Men

Low testosterone affects an estimated 1 in 4 men over 30. Symptoms include fatigue, brain fog, decreased libido, weight gain, and loss of muscle mass. Our TRT Membership program includes comprehensive lab work, ongoing monitoring, and personalized dosing — all managed by a naturopathic physician who understands the full picture of men's health.

Safety and Monitoring

Hormone therapy is not "one size fits all." Proper management requires regular lab monitoring, symptom tracking, and dose adjustments. We monitor not just hormone levels but also metabolites, liver function, and cardiovascular markers to ensure your therapy is both effective and safe.

Is BHRT Right for You?

If you're experiencing symptoms of hormonal imbalance — whether you're 35 or 65 — a comprehensive hormone evaluation can determine if BHRT could help you feel like yourself again. Learn more about our hormone replacement services including Biote Pellets, Wiley Protocol, and rhythmic hormone therapy.

title: IV Therapy: Beyond the Basics
excerpt: Explore the science behind IV nutrient therapy and how it can support your health goals more effectively than oral supplements.
content:
IV therapy has gone mainstream — but at Reclaim Integrative Medicine, we go far beyond the basic "vitamin drip." Our IV therapy program uses medical-grade formulations designed to address specific health conditions, not just general wellness.

Why IV Over Oral Supplements?

When you take nutrients orally, your body absorbs only a fraction — typically 20-40% depending on your gut health. IV administration delivers nutrients directly into your bloodstream at 100% bioavailability, bypassing the digestive system entirely.

This matters especially for patients with:

  • Digestive issues that impair nutrient absorption
  • Chronic conditions requiring therapeutic (higher) nutrient doses
  • Acute illness or recovery needs
  • Mitochondrial dysfunction or chronic fatigue

NAD+ IV Therapy

NAD+ (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide) is a coenzyme found in every cell of your body. It's essential for energy production, DNA repair, and cellular communication. NAD+ levels naturally decline with age — by age 50, you may have only half the NAD+ you had at 20.

NAD+ IV therapy has been shown to support:

  • Cellular energy production — combat fatigue at the mitochondrial level
  • Cognitive clarity — reduce brain fog and improve focus
  • Anti-aging — activate sirtuins, the "longevity genes"
  • Addiction recovery — support neurotransmitter balance
  • Athletic performance — faster recovery and improved endurance

Plaquex IV Therapy

Plaquex (phosphatidylcholine) is a powerful IV therapy primarily used for cardiovascular and liver health. Phosphatidylcholine is a major component of cell membranes and plays a critical role in lipid metabolism.

Plaquex IV therapy can help with:

  • Reducing arterial plaque — supports healthy cholesterol metabolism
  • Liver detoxification — protects hepatocytes and supports liver cell regeneration
  • Cell membrane repair — improves cellular function throughout the body
  • Neurological support — phosphatidylcholine is critical for brain health

Mitochondrial IV Therapy

Our Mitochondrial IV is specifically formulated to support your body's energy powerhouses. This infusion typically includes a combination of:

  • B vitamins (especially B1, B2, B3, and B5)
  • Magnesium
  • Alpha-lipoic acid
  • L-carnitine
  • CoQ10 support nutrients

This protocol is especially beneficial for patients with chronic fatigue, fibromyalgia, or anyone looking to optimize their cellular energy production.

What to Expect During Your IV Session

IV sessions at our Newport Beach office typically last 30-90 minutes depending on the formula. You'll relax in a comfortable treatment room while the nutrients are slowly infused. Many patients read, work on their laptops, or simply rest during their session.

Most patients report feeling increased energy, mental clarity, and an overall sense of well-being within hours of their infusion.

Is IV Therapy Right for You?

IV therapy works best as part of a comprehensive treatment plan. During your consultation, we'll review your labs, health history, and goals to determine which IV protocols would benefit you most. Explore our full range of IV therapy services, or read about NAD+ therapy options including IV, injectable, and nasal spray.

title: Managing Chronic Fatigue: A Naturopathic Approach
excerpt: Struggling with persistent tiredness? Learn about the root causes of chronic fatigue and integrative strategies for recovery.
content:
"I'm just tired all the time." It's one of the most common complaints we hear at Reclaim Integrative Medicine — and one of the most dismissed by conventional medicine. If you've been told your labs are "normal" but you're still dragging through every day, you're not imagining it. There's always a reason.

Fatigue Is a Symptom, Not a Diagnosis

Chronic fatigue is your body's way of telling you something isn't working. The challenge is that fatigue can stem from dozens of different root causes — and most conventional workups only scratch the surface.

Common underlying causes we investigate include:

  • Thyroid dysfunction — even "subclinical" hypothyroidism can cause profound fatigue (learn more)
  • Adrenal dysfunction — chronic stress depletes cortisol and DHEA
  • Iron deficiency — ferritin can be "low normal" but still cause fatigue
  • Mitochondrial dysfunction — your cells can't produce enough energy
  • Chronic infections — Epstein-Barr virus reactivation, Lyme disease, mold exposure
  • Hormonal imbalances — low testosterone, estrogen fluctuations, progesterone deficiency
  • Blood sugar dysregulation — insulin resistance causes energy crashes
  • Nutrient deficiencies — B12, vitamin D, magnesium, CoQ10
  • Sleep disorders — including undiagnosed sleep apnea
  • Gut dysbiosis — poor gut health impairs nutrient absorption and increases systemic inflammation

Our Comprehensive Fatigue Workup

Our chronic fatigue program starts with an extensive evaluation that goes far beyond a CBC and metabolic panel. We typically order:

  • Complete thyroid panel with antibodies
  • Adrenal cortisol testing (salivary or DUTCH)
  • Complete iron studies including ferritin
  • Full hormone panel
  • Inflammatory markers (hs-CRP, ESR)
  • Viral titers (EBV, CMV, HHV-6)
  • Nutrient levels (B12, folate, vitamin D, magnesium RBC)
  • Metabolic markers including fasting insulin and HbA1c
  • Mold and environmental toxin screening when indicated

Treatment: Addressing the Root Cause

Once we identify what's driving your fatigue, treatment is targeted and personalized. Common interventions include:

  • Targeted supplementation — repleting specific nutrient deficiencies
  • IV therapy — Mitochondrial IVs and NAD+ for cellular energy support
  • Alpha lipoic acid therapy — ALA IV and IM injections to recharge mitochondrial cofactors and reduce oxidative stress
  • Hormone optimization — restoring thyroid, adrenal, and sex hormones to optimal levels
  • Gut restoration — addressing dysbiosis, SIBO, or infections
  • Anti-viral protocols — for reactivated chronic infections
  • Lifestyle medicine — sleep hygiene, stress management, and movement programming
  • B12 injections — our Mega B Injections deliver energy-boosting B vitamins directly

How Long Until I Feel Better?

Every patient is different, but most begin to notice improvements within 2-4 weeks of starting a targeted treatment plan. More complex cases — especially those involving mold exposure or chronic infections — may take longer, but the key is that we're addressing the cause, not just masking the symptom.

You Deserve More Than "Normal" Labs

If you're in Newport Beach, Orange County, or the Inland Empire and you're tired of being tired, we can help you find answers. Learn more about our chronic fatigue services and how IV therapy can support your recovery. Your fatigue has a cause — and it's treatable.

title: The Power of Comprehensive Lab Testing
excerpt: Why standard lab panels often miss the full picture and how comprehensive testing can uncover hidden health issues.
content:
You've probably heard the frustrating phrase: "Your labs look normal." But if everything is "normal," why don't you feel normal? At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, we believe the problem isn't you — it's the labs being ordered.

Standard Labs vs. Comprehensive Labs

A typical annual physical includes a basic metabolic panel, CBC, and maybe a lipid panel and TSH. While these tests have value, they represent a tiny fraction of the information available about your health.

Here's what standard testing often misses:

  • Thyroid antibodies — Hashimoto's can be developing for years before TSH becomes abnormal
  • Fasting insulin — you can be insulin resistant with a "normal" glucose
  • Ferritin — iron storage can be depleted while your hemoglobin looks fine
  • Vitamin D — deficiency affects immunity, mood, bones, and hormones
  • Inflammatory markers — hs-CRP and homocysteine reveal cardiovascular and systemic inflammation
  • Full hormone panels — estradiol, progesterone, testosterone, DHEA-S, and cortisol
  • Nutrient levels — B12, folate, magnesium RBC, zinc

"Normal" vs. Optimal

There's a critical distinction between normal lab ranges and optimal ranges. Normal ranges are based on population averages — including sick people. Optimal ranges represent where your markers should be for you to feel your best.

For example:

  • Vitamin D: Lab "normal" starts at 30 ng/mL, but optimal function often requires 50-80 ng/mL
  • Ferritin: Lab "normal" can be as low as 12 ng/mL, but symptoms of deficiency often persist below 50 ng/mL
  • TSH: Lab range goes up to 4.5, but many patients feel best between 1.0-2.0
  • Fasting insulin: Often not even tested, but values above 7-8 suggest early insulin resistance

Specialty Testing We Offer

Beyond blood work, our comprehensive lab program includes access to advanced functional testing:

  • DUTCH test — comprehensive hormone and adrenal metabolite analysis
  • GI-MAP stool testing — PCR-based gut pathogen and microbiome assessment
  • Organic acids testing (OAT) — metabolic, mitochondrial, and microbial markers
  • Food sensitivity panels — IgG-mediated delayed food reactions
  • Mycotoxin testing — for patients with suspected mold exposure
  • Heavy metals testing — provoked or unprovoked urine analysis
  • Genetic testing — MTHFR, detoxification SNPs, and nutrigenomics

Why Testing Matters for Your Treatment Plan

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, we don't guess — we test. Every treatment recommendation we make is grounded in objective data. This approach means:

  • We know exactly what to treat (no shotgun approach)
  • We can track your progress with follow-up labs
  • We can adjust dosing based on your body's actual response
  • You understand why you're taking what you're taking

Get the Full Picture

If you're ready to look beyond "normal" and find out what's really going on, explore our comprehensive lab testing services. Our team in Newport Beach and Rancho Cucamonga is here to help. Comprehensive testing is the first step toward truly personalized medicine.

title: EBOO Therapy: The Most Advanced Blood Oxygenation Treatment Available
excerpt: Learn how EBOO (Extracorporeal Blood Oxygenation and Ozonation) therapy detoxifies blood, reduces inflammation, and supports healing for chronic fatigue, autoimmune conditions, and more.
content:
If you've been dealing with chronic fatigue, persistent inflammation, autoimmune flare-ups, or the lingering effects of infections like Lyme disease or COVID-19, you may have heard about EBOO therapy — one of the most powerful detoxification and oxygenation treatments available in integrative medicine today.

What Exactly Is EBOO?

EBOO stands for Extracorporeal Blood Oxygenation and Ozonation. Unlike standard ozone therapies that treat a small volume of blood, EBOO filters and oxygenates your entire blood supply through a specialized dialysis-like membrane outside the body.

During a session, blood is gently drawn from one arm, passed through a medical-grade filter where it's exposed to ozone (O₃) and pure oxygen, then returned cleansed and revitalized through the other arm. The entire process takes approximately 45–60 minutes.

How EBOO Differs from Other Ozone Therapies

Traditional ozone therapies — such as Major Autohemotherapy (MAH) — treat roughly 200–250 mL of blood per session. EBOO processes significantly more, filtering up to 3–5 liters during a single treatment. This means a far more comprehensive detoxification effect.

The dialysis-style filter also physically removes toxins, dead cells, heavy metals, and inflammatory byproducts that other ozone methods simply cannot address. Many patients can visibly see the difference in the filter after treatment — a stark reminder of what the body accumulates over time.

The Science Behind EBOO

Ozone therapy works by creating a controlled oxidative stress response. When medical-grade ozone contacts your blood, it triggers several beneficial cascades:

  • Enhanced oxygen delivery: Ozone improves the ability of red blood cells to release oxygen to tissues, addressing cellular hypoxia that underlies many chronic conditions.
  • Immune modulation: Ozone stimulates the production of cytokines and interferons, helping regulate an overactive or underperforming immune system.
  • Antimicrobial action: Ozone disrupts the cell membranes of bacteria, viruses, and fungi — making it effective against chronic infections.
  • Mitochondrial activation: By increasing oxygen availability, EBOO supports mitochondrial function — the energy powerhouses of every cell.
  • Reduced oxidative stress: Paradoxically, the controlled oxidative burst from ozone upregulates the body's own antioxidant pathways (glutathione, superoxide dismutase).

Who Can Benefit from EBOO?

EBOO therapy is particularly effective for patients dealing with:

  • Chronic fatigue syndrome and persistent low energy
  • Autoimmune conditions including Hashimoto's, lupus, and rheumatoid arthritis
  • Chronic infections such as Lyme disease, Epstein-Barr virus, and co-infections
  • Mold toxicity and environmental illness
  • Post-COVID syndrome (long COVID) with brain fog, fatigue, and inflammation
  • Cardiovascular concerns including poor circulation and high inflammatory markers
  • Neurological symptoms including brain fog, neuropathy, and cognitive decline
  • Heavy metal and toxin accumulation from environmental exposures

What to Expect During Treatment

An EBOO session at Reclaim Integrative Medicine is performed in a comfortable clinical setting under medical supervision. Here's what a typical visit looks like:

  1. Pre-treatment assessment: We review your labs, health history, and treatment goals to ensure EBOO is appropriate for you.
  2. IV access: Two IV lines are placed — one to draw blood and one to return it. The process is similar to donating blood.
  3. Filtration and ozonation: Your blood circulates through the EBOO device for 45–60 minutes while you relax.
  4. Post-treatment: Most patients feel a noticeable boost in energy and mental clarity within hours. Some experience mild detox symptoms for 24–48 hours.

How Many Sessions Are Recommended?

The number of sessions depends on your condition and health goals. Many patients notice improvement after a single treatment, but a series of 3–6 sessions spaced 1–2 weeks apart typically delivers the most significant results. Maintenance sessions every 4–8 weeks can help sustain benefits long-term.

Combining EBOO with Other Therapies

EBOO pairs exceptionally well with other treatments we offer at Reclaim Integrative Medicine:

  • NAD+ IV therapy: Combining EBOO with NAD+ amplifies mitochondrial support and cellular repair.
  • Peptide therapy: BPC-157 and TB-500 can accelerate tissue healing alongside EBOO's oxygenation benefits.
  • Comprehensive lab testing: We use advanced labs to track inflammatory markers, oxidative stress, and immune function before and after treatment.
  • IV nutrient therapy: High-dose vitamin C, glutathione, and mineral infusions complement the detoxification process.

Is EBOO Safe?

EBOO is a well-tolerated procedure when performed by trained medical professionals using FDA-cleared equipment. Side effects are typically mild and may include temporary fatigue, mild headache, or detox-like symptoms as the body processes released toxins. These typically resolve within 24–48 hours.

At Reclaim Integrative Medicine, every EBOO session is supervised by our medical team to ensure safety and optimal outcomes.

EBOO Treatment Packages — Save Up to 15%

Because EBOO therapy delivers the best results with a series of treatments, we offer multi-session packages to make advanced healing more accessible:

  • Single Session: $1,300
  • 3-Session Package: $3,510 ($1,170/session — save $390)
  • 6-Session Package: $6,630 ($1,105/session — save $1,170)

Most patients begin to notice improvements after 2–3 sessions, with optimal results typically seen over a full 6-session protocol. Your provider will recommend the right treatment frequency based on your condition and goals.

Take the Next Step

If you're searching for answers to chronic health challenges that conventional medicine hasn't resolved, EBOO therapy may be the missing piece. By deeply detoxifying your blood, enhancing oxygen delivery, and activating your body's innate healing mechanisms, EBOO offers a level of therapeutic support that few other treatments can match.

View our EBOO therapy packages or explore how EBOO pairs with IV therapy and peptide therapy for comprehensive healing.
